caption: No-teleport soldier collisions. The per-soldier position-correction separation pass is retired -- soldiers never snap apart. Spacing now emerges from velocity instead: friendly crowding is handled by a behavioural avoidance pass (each soldier steers off a neighbour it would overlap), and enemy contact by combat KNOCKBACK -- a landed blow shoves the struck soldier back, and a charging hit throws him further. As the two infantry blocks clash, the contact line presses in and recoils as a live balance of push vs. knockback rather than holding a fixed gap, and every body moves at its own velocity (no snapping). This sets up the emergent spear-vs-sword standoff (#240) and is the next step of the individual-collision pillar (#164). Knockback constants are a first cut, tuned against playtests.
